entities.name: CROP
entities.description: General term for cultivated plants used for food or other purposes

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Measurable characteristics of a crop that determine its agricultural value

entities.name: STRESS
entities.description: External pressures that negatively affect crop growth and development

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming practices that meet current food needs without compromising future generations

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns

entities.name: GENETIC IDENTIFICATION
entities.description: The process of locating specific genetic sequences associated with traits

entities.name: CHARACTERISATION
entities.description: The process of describing the features and properties of genetic traits

entities.name: QTL MAPPING
entities.description: Quantitative Trait Locus mapping used to link phenotypic traits to genomic regions

entities.name: GWAS
entities.description: Genome-wide association studies used to associate genetic variants with traits

entities.name: MAS
entities.description: Marker-assisted selection using molecular markers to select for desired traits

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A form of MAS that uses all available markers across the genome

entities.name: FUNCTIONAL GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of how genes and genomes interact to produce phenotypes

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of RNA transcripts produced by the genome

entities.name: PROTEOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of proteins

entities.name: METABOLOMICS
entities.description: The study of small molecule metabolites

entities.name: GENOMIC REGIONS
entities.description: Specific segments of DNA associated with particular traits or responses

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural product harvested per unit area

entities.name: QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional or commercial value of the crop product

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by living organisms such as pests or pathogens

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by non-living factors such as drought or salinity

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The improvement in the genetic merit of a crop population over time

entities.name: CROP BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Systematic efforts to develop improved crop varieties

entities.name: CULTIVARS
entities.description: Cultivated varieties of plants with specific desirable traits

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that damage crops

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
entities.description: External physical or chemical conditions affecting plant growth

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species

entities.name: GERMPLASM RESOURCES
entities.description: The sum total of all the genetic resources available for a crop

entities.name: MOLECULAR BREEDING STRATEGIES
entities.description: The use of molecular tools to improve crop breeding efficiency
